prompt: Surreal conceptual scene expressing Simpson's Paradox through spatial contradiction. Two separate winding rivers flow downhill through two different valleys, each clearly descending toward lower ground. When viewed individually they appear to flow in the same direction. Both rivers eventually merge into one large river that unexpectedly flows upward toward a mountain peak, creating an impossible global direction that contradicts the local flows. Landscape composition emphasizing local vs global structure: split valleys with consistent downward slopes, merging into a single contradictory upward current. Dreamlike natural environment, smooth atmospheric lighting, subtle visual cues guiding the eye from the two valleys toward the merged impossible river. No text, no numbers, no charts, no symbols.
visual_logic: Two valleys each slope downward with rivers flowing down. When the rivers merge, the combined river flows upward, visually embodying how local trends reverse when groups are combined.
